I agree with the consensus that the correct answer is \( \text{B} \). My additional rationale focuses on reinforcing how the family history provides critical clues regarding the mode of inheritance. Since both maternal and paternal uncles show similar symptoms, suggesting an autosomal dominant or X-linked pattern, but considering Hemophilia primarily follows an X-linked recessive model, it strengthens our confidence in diagnosing Hemophilia B over other possibilities. This comprehensive understanding ensures accurate identification and appropriate management strategies moving forward.

Additional Analysis:
Given the strong family history showing affected individuals on both maternal and paternal sides, coupled with classical symptoms like joint hemorrhages and gum bleeding, Hemophilia B remains the most plausible explanation.
